sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.91 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a finite group with an abelian Sylow$p$-subgroup$A$. Suppose that$B$is a strongly closed elementary abelian subgroup of$A$. Without invoking the Classi\-fication Theorem for Finite Simple Groups (CFSG), prove that$G$has a normal subgroup$N$such that$B = _1(A N)$. For$p = 2$, this is a corollary of a theorem of Goldschmidt. For$p$odd, this has been proved by Flores and Foote (Adv. Math., {...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
